title: Monitoring Attention in Self and Others.

assets.abstract: The ability of the brain to monitor its own attention is important for controlling attention. The ability to reconstruct and monitor the attention of others is important for behavioral prediction and therefore interaction with others. Do the same cortical networks participate in constructing a metacognitive representation of attention, whether one's own or someone else's attention? We studied the brain activity of human participants in an fMRI scanner. The participants performed two attention-mo

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.
